# Deploy ForgeFlowKit to forgeflowkit.com (Cloudflare Pages + Plausible) Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Take ForgeFlowKit from "built locally" to "live at forgeflowkit.com with privacy-friendly analytics" — enabling real-world SEO and traffic data to drive future product decisions.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by Astro 4.16.19 (141 HTML pages). Deployed to Cloudflare Pages via a GitHub repo (Gitee is the current primary remote; Gitee mirrors to GitHub automatically via Gitee's built-in mirror feature). Plausible Analytics for GDPR-friendly traffic tracking. Domain `forgeflowkit.com` purchased via Cloudflare Registrar (lowest friction — same dashboard for DNS).

**Tech Stack:** Astro 4.16.19 (static, no SSR) · Cloudflare Pages (hosting + CDN) · Cloudflare Registrar (domain) · Plausible Analytics (privacy-friendly analytics) · GitHub Actions + Gitee mirror (repo sync) · @astrojs/sitemap (already configured).

**Pre-flight verified 2026-06-22:**
- ✅ `astro.config.mjs` has `site: 'https://forgeflowkit.com'` (line 6)
- ✅ `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` exists with `<head>` section ready for analytics script
- ✅ `@astrojs/sitemap` integration configured (sitemaps auto-generated at build)
- ✅ `pnpm build` produces 141 pages
- ✅ Git remote is `gitee.com:wlz679/calcKit.git` only (no GitHub yet)
- ✅ Master branch, working tree clean, pushed to Gitee

---

## Task 1: Add Plausible analytics to BaseLayout

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro:28-29` (insert after `<link rel="icon" ...>` line, before `{schema && ...}` line)

**Why:** Plausible gives GDPR-friendly traffic analytics without cookie banners. Single `<script>` tag, ~1 KB, no impact on CWV.

- [ ] **Step 1.1: Insert Plausible script into BaseLayout**

Find the line `<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />` in `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` and add immediately after it (before the `{schema && ...}` line):

```astro
<script defer data-domain="forgeflowkit.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>
```

The resulting `<head>` block (lines 17-30) should look like:

```astro
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>{title}</title>
  <meta name="description" content={description} />
  <meta property="og:title" content={title} />
  <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
  <meta property="og:image" content={ogImage} />
  <meta property="og:type" content="website" />
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow" />
  <link rel="sitemap" href="/sitemap-index.xml" />
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
  <script defer data-domain="forgeflowkit.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>
  {schema && <script type="application/ld+json" set:html={schema} />}
</head>
```

- [ ] **Step 1.2: Build and verify the script tag is present in dist/**

Run: `pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -5`
Expected: "141 page(s) built" — build succeeds.

Then run: `grep -l "plausible.io/js/script.js" dist/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator/index.html`
Expected: prints the file path (Plausible script is in the rendered output).

- [ ] **Step 1.3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro dist/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator/index.html
# (other dist pages also change — git add -u picks them up)
git add -u dist/
git commit -m "feat(analytics): add Plausible script to BaseLayout (deferred, 1KB, GDPR-friendly)"
```

---

## Task 2: Add Cloudflare Pages security headers

**Files:**
- Create: `public/_headers`

**Why:** `_headers` is a Cloudflare Pages convention (also works on Netlify). Lets us set security + cache headers without per-page config. Without this, the site ships with zero CSP / X-Frame-Options / etc.

- [ ] **Step 2.1: Create `public/_headers`**

Create file `public/_headers` with the following content (Unix LF line endings):

```
/*
  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
  Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
  Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
  Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
  # Astro already inlines critical CSS, so cache HTML aggressively
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate

/_astro/*
  # Astro fingerprint hashes content; cache aggressively (1 year)
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable

/sitemap-index.xml
  /sitemap-0.xml
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600

/favicon.svg
  /og-default.png
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400
```

Note: the `_headers` syntax is path-glob + indented header lines. Cloudflare Pages reads this file from `public/` at deploy time.

- [ ] **Step 2.2: Build and verify _headers is copied to dist**

Run: `pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -3`
Then: `ls dist/_headers`
Expected: file exists in dist root.

- [ ] **Step 2.3: Commit**

```bash
git add public/_headers dist/_headers
git commit -m "feat(deploy): add Cloudflare Pages _headers (security + cache)"
```

---

## Task 3: Add www → apex redirect (canonical domain enforcement)

**Files:**
- Create: `public/_redirects`

**Why:** SEO best practice — pick one canonical domain (apex `forgeflowkit.com`) and 301 redirect the other (`www.forgeflowkit.com`). Prevents duplicate-content penalty.

- [ ] **Step 3.1: Create `public/_redirects`**

Create file `public/_redirects`:

```
# www → apex (301 permanent)
https://www.forgeflowkit.com/* https://forgeflowkit.com/:splat 301

# Force HTTPS (Cloudflare Pages does this automatically, but explicit is safer)
/http://* https://forgeflowkit.com/:splat 301
```

The first rule handles `www.forgeflowkit.com` → `forgeflowkit.com`. The second is a safety net for `http://` URLs (Cloudflare usually handles this but belt-and-suspenders).

- [ ] **Step 3.2: Build and verify _redirects is copied**

Run: `pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -3`
Then: `cat dist/_redirects`
Expected: shows both redirect rules.

- [ ] **Step 3.3: Commit**

```bash
git add public/_redirects dist/_redirects
git commit -m "feat(deploy): add www→apex 301 redirect via Cloudflare Pages _redirects"
```

---

## Task 4: User buys forgeflowkit.com domain

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — requires Cloudflare account and payment method. Cannot be automated.

**Files:** None (DNS config happens in Task 7).

- [ ] **Step 4.1: Create Cloudflare account (if not already)**

Go to https://dash.cloudflare.com/sign-up and sign up. Free tier is sufficient.

- [ ] **Step 4.2: Register forgeflowkit.com via Cloudflare Registrar**

1. In Cloudflare dashboard, click "Register Domains" (left sidebar).
2. Search for `forgeflowkit.com`. If available, add to cart.
3. Cost: ~$10-12/year (at-cost, no markup — Cloudflare Registrar is the cheapest option).
4. Payment: credit card / PayPal.
5. Choose registration term: 1 year (renewable).

If `forgeflowkit.com` is already taken, stop and report to developer — this plan assumes it's available. Alternatives:
- Try `calckit.app`, `getforgeflowkit.com`, `calckit.io`
- Each would require updating `astro.config.mjs` `site:` field + Plausible `data-domain` attribute

- [ ] **Step 4.3: Confirm domain is added to Cloudflare**

After purchase, the domain should appear in Cloudflare dashboard → "Websites" list. DNS is automatically managed by Cloudflare.

---

## Task 5: Create GitHub repo + configure Gitee mirror

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — requires GitHub account.

**Files:** None (mirror is configured in Gitee's web UI).

Cloudflare Pages integrates with GitHub (not Gitee directly). Strategy: keep Gitee as primary remote, mirror to GitHub via Gitee's built-in mirror feature.

- [ ] **Step 5.1: Create empty GitHub repo**

1. Go to https://github.com/new
2. Repository name: `calcKit` (or your preferred name)
3. Visibility: **Public** (Cloudflare Pages free tier requires public repo for static site deploys)
4. **Do NOT** initialize with README, .gitignore, or license (Gitee repo already has these)
5. Click "Create repository"

- [ ] **Step 5.2: Configure Gitee → GitHub mirror**

1. Go to https://gitee.com/wlz679/calcKit (the existing Gitee repo)
2. Click "管理" (Management) → "仓库设置" (Repo settings) → "镜像管理" (Mirror management)
3. Under "镜像到 GitHub", click "添加镜像" (Add mirror)
4. Paste the GitHub repo URL: `https://github.com/<your-username>/calcKit.git`
5. Authentication: provide GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope
6. Enable "Push mirror" (主动推送镜像)
7. Click "确定" (Confirm)

After this, every `git push gitee master` will automatically push to GitHub as well.

- [ ] **Step 5.3: Verify mirror works**

After completing Task 4 (and pushing the new commits), check:
- GitHub repo `https://github.com/<your-username>/calcKit` should have the latest commits.
- This may take 1-2 minutes after the Gitee push to propagate.

If mirror isn't working, fall back to manual mirror:
```bash
git remote add github https://github.com/<your-username>/calcKit.git
git push github master
```

---

## Task 6: Connect Cloudflare Pages to GitHub repo

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — done in Cloudflare dashboard.

**Files:** None (Cloudflare config is web-UI only).

- [ ] **Step 6.1: Create Cloudflare Pages project**

1. In Cloudflare dashboard, click "Workers & Pages" → "Create" → "Pages" tab → "Connect to Git"
2. Select GitHub, authorize Cloudflare to access the repo.
3. Pick the `calcKit` repo.
4. Click "Begin setup"

- [ ] **Step 6.2: Configure build settings**

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Project name | `calckit` (becomes `calckit.pages.dev` for the preview URL) |
| Production branch | `master` |
| Framework preset | `Astro` (Cloudflare auto-detects) |
| Build command | `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build` |
| Build output directory | `dist` |
| Root directory | (leave empty) |
| Environment variables | `NODE_VERSION` = `20` |
| | `PNPM_VERSION` = `9` (or your pinned version) |

Why `--frozen-lockfile`: ensures Cloudflare's build matches the lockfile committed to the repo (prevents drift).

- [ ] **Step 6.3: Save and Deploy**

Click "Save and Deploy". First build takes 2-3 minutes. Cloudflare shows build logs in real-time.

Expected: "Success! Your site is deployed to https://calckit.pages.dev" (preview URL, not yet custom domain).

- [ ] **Step 6.4: Verify preview URL works**

Open `https://calckit.pages.dev` in a browser. Should show ForgeFlowKit landing page.
Open `https://calckit.pages.dev/sitemap-index.xml` — should show sitemap index.
Open `https://calckit.pages.dev/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator` — should show CAC calculator.

If any page fails, check the build log in Cloudflare dashboard. Most common issue: missing `NODE_VERSION` env var → Cloudflare uses old Node which doesn't support Astro 4.

---

## Task 7: Add forgeflowkit.com as custom domain

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — done in Cloudflare dashboard.

**Files:** None (DNS is auto-managed since domain is registered with Cloudflare).

- [ ] **Step 7.1: Add custom domain to Pages project**

1. In Cloudflare Pages project dashboard, click "Custom domains" tab.
2. Click "Set up a custom domain"
3. Enter `forgeflowkit.com` → Click "Continue"
4. Since domain is on Cloudflare DNS, the CNAME is auto-added. Click "Activate domain"
5. (Optional) Add `www.forgeflowkit.com` → it will redirect to apex via the `_redirects` rule from Task 3.

- [ ] **Step 7.2: Verify HTTPS works**

After 1-2 minutes (DNS + SSL provisioning):
- Open `https://forgeflowkit.com` — should show ForgeFlowKit with valid SSL (no browser warning).
- Open `https://www.forgeflowkit.com` — should redirect (301) to `https://forgeflowkit.com`.
- Click the padlock icon → should show "Connection is secure" issued by Cloudflare.

- [ ] **Step 7.3: Verify Plausible is tracking**

1. Open `https://forgeflowkit.com` in a browser
2. Open browser DevTools → Network tab → reload
3. Should see a request to `https://plausible.io/js/script.js` (200 OK)
4. Navigate to a calculator page (e.g., `/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator`) → should see another Plausible request (pageview)
5. Go to https://plausible.io/forgeflowkit.com (your Plausible dashboard) → wait 5-10 seconds → should show the pageviews

If Plausible shows nothing: clear browser cache, disable ad blockers (Plausible is sometimes blocked by privacy extensions).

---

## Task 8: Submit sitemap to Search Console + Bing

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — requires Google + Bing accounts.

**Files:** None (search console verification is web-UI only).

- [ ] **Step 8.1: Google Search Console**

1. Go to https://search.google.com/search-console/
2. Click "Add property" → "URL prefix" → enter `https://forgeflowkit.com`
3. Verification method: **HTML tag** (simplest)
   - Cloudflare auto-injects meta tags? No — we need to add a `<meta name="google-site-verification" content="..." />` tag to BaseLayout.
   - OR use **DNS TXT record** method (add TXT record to Cloudflare DNS — verified automatically)
   - DNS method preferred (no code change)
4. After verification, go to "Sitemaps" (left sidebar) → enter `sitemap-index.xml` → click "Submit"
5. Wait 24-48 hours for Google to crawl. Check "Pages" report to see indexed pages.

- [ ] **Step 8.2: Bing Webmaster Tools**

1. Go to https://www.bing.com/webmasters
2. "Add a site" → enter `https://forgeflowkit.com`
3. Verification: same as Google (DNS TXT record works for both — add the same record)
4. Submit sitemap: `https://forgeflowkit.com/sitemap-index.xml`

- [ ] **Step 8.3: Verify hreflang tags**

The site has en/zh pages. Verify the `hreflang` setup is correct:
- `curl -s https://forgeflowkit.com/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator | grep -i hreflang`
- Expected: shows `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://forgeflowkit.com/en/...">` and `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="zh" href="https://forgeflowkit.com/zh/...">`

If hreflang tags are missing, that's a separate Astro config issue — file a follow-up plan to add `@astrojs/i18n` integration. For now, en and zh are still indexable separately.

---

## Task 9: Lighthouse audit + fix critical issues

**Files:** Varies based on findings (likely no changes if pre-flight checks pass).

- [ ] **Step 9.1: Run Lighthouse against production URL**

Using Chrome DevTools:
1. Open `https://forgeflowkit.com/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator` (most representative page)
2. DevTools → Lighthouse tab
3. Select: Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO
4. Mode: Mobile
5. Click "Analyze page load"

Expected scores (targets):
- Performance: ≥ 90
- Accessibility: ≥ 95
- Best Practices: ≥ 95
- SEO: = 100 (we already have og:tags, meta description, sitemap, robots)

- [ ] **Step 9.2: Fix any issues found**

Common issues and fixes (if any):
- **Performance < 90**: Likely the bar chart in `ai-api-cost-comparison` or model list in `openai-token-calculator` shipping too much JS. May need code splitting. Out of scope for this plan — file a follow-up.
- **Accessibility < 95**: Likely color contrast on tier indicators (🟢🟡🟠🔴). May need text labels (🟢 Healthy). Out of scope — file follow-up.
- **Best Practices < 95**: Check console for errors. Common cause: missing `<meta name="theme-color">`. Easy fix in BaseLayout.
- **SEO < 100**: Almost certainly will be 100 since we have og:tags, structured data, sitemap, robots meta.

Document any < 90 score in a follow-up issue. Do NOT block deployment on this — ship and iterate.
